title: Payment Outage Runbook — Detection, Triage, Escalation, Recovery, and Communications
content:
Payment Outage Runbook

Purpose: Step-by-step procedures when payment processing is degraded or unavailable.

1. DETECTION
- Monitor payment latency P95, error rate, and queue depth dashboards (Grafana: Payments-Prod).
- Alert triggers: P95 latency > 2s for 5 minutes, error rate > 1%, or zero successful authorizations for 3 minutes.
- Confirm impact via synthetic transaction probe (run: pay-probe --env prod).

2. TRIAGE
- Classify severity using Payment SLA Policy (see PAY-SLA-001).
- Check recent deployments (last 2 hours) in payments-api and payment-gateway services.
- Inspect database connection pool metrics: active connections, wait time, pool exhaustion errors.
- Review incident history for recurring themes (e.g., connection pool saturation under peak load).

3. ESCALATION
- Sev-1: Page Payments On-Call immediately; notify Incident Commander within 10 minutes.
- Sev-2: Slack #payments-incidents; on-call acknowledges within 15 minutes.
- Escalation matrix: L1 On-Call → L2 Payments Engineering → L3 Platform/DBA → VP Payments.
- Open incident ticket in ServiceNow with category PAYMENTS-OUTAGE.

4. RECOVERY
- If connection pool exhausted: scale payments-api replicas (+2), increase pool max_size per Database Pool Tuning Guide.
- Drain stuck transactions from dead-letter queue; replay after root cause mitigated.
- Fail over to secondary payment processor if primary gateway unreachable > 10 minutes.
- Validate recovery: run pay-probe suite; confirm P95 latency < 500ms and error rate < 0.1% for 15 minutes.

5. COMMUNICATIONS
- Internal: post status to #payments-incidents every 15 minutes during active outage.
- Customer-facing: coordinate with Comms team; use approved status page template for payment delays.
- Executive briefing for Sev-1 within 30 minutes of declaration.
- Post-incident: schedule blameless postmortem within 48 hours using Postmortem Template.

Related documents: Payment SLA Policy, Database Pool Tuning Guide, Escalation Matrix, On-Call Handbook.
